principles.name: Perceivable
principles.description: Information and user interface components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive.

guidelines.name: Text Alternatives
guidelines.description: Provide text alternatives for any non-text content.

criteria.name: Non-text Content
criteria.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.
criteria.notes: Automated tools can detect missing alt attributes but cannot assess alt text quality.

criteria.name: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: For prerecorded audio-only and video-only media, an alternative is provided.
criteria.notes: Requires human review to verify transcript accuracy and completeness.

criteria.name: Captions (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.
criteria.notes: Automated tools can detect presence of captions but not accuracy, synchronisation, or speaker identification.

criteria.name: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: An alternative for time-based media or audio description is provided for prerecorded video content.
criteria.notes: Requires human evaluation to assess whether visual information is conveyed.

criteria.name: Captions (Live)
criteria.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.
criteria.notes: Live captions must be evaluated in real time. Cannot be automated.

criteria.name: Audio Description (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content.
criteria.notes: Requires human review to verify audio description quality and completeness.

criteria.name: Sign Language (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content.
criteria.notes: AAA criterion. Requires human evaluation.

criteria.name: Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: Extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content.
criteria.notes: AAA criterion.

criteria.name: Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
criteria.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media.
criteria.notes: AAA criterion.

criteria.name: Audio-only (Live)
criteria.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for live audio-only content.
criteria.notes: AAA criterion. Live content only.

guidelines.name: Adaptable
guidelines.description: Create content that can be presented in different ways without losing information.

criteria.name: Info and Relationships
criteria.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined.
criteria.notes: Automated tools can check semantic HTML but cannot fully assess logical structure.

criteria.name: Meaningful Sequence
criteria.description: If the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, the correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

criteria.name: Sensory Characteristics
criteria.description: Instructions do not rely solely on sensory characteristics such as shape, color, size, visual location, or sound.
criteria.notes: Requires human review to identify sensory-only instructions.

criteria.name: Orientation
criteria.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation.

criteria.name: Identify Input Purpose
criteria.description: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined.
criteria.notes: Can be checked by verifying autocomplete attributes.

criteria.name: Identify Purpose
criteria.description: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of UI components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.
criteria.notes: AAA criterion.

guidelines.name: Distinguishable
guidelines.description: Make it easier for users to see and hear content.

criteria.name: Use of Color
criteria.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information.
criteria.notes: Automated tools can flag color-only patterns but human review is needed for context.

criteria.name: Audio Control
criteria.description: If any audio plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, a mechanism to pause or stop it is available.
criteria.notes: Presence of autoplay can be detected; control availability needs verification.

criteria.name: Contrast (Minimum)
criteria.description: Text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background.
criteria.notes: Well-supported by automated tools such as axe-core.

criteria.name: Resize Text
criteria.description: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality.
criteria.notes: Requires manual browser zoom testing.

criteria.name: Images of Text
criteria.description: Text is used instead of images of text where possible.

criteria.name: Reflow
criteria.description: Content can be presented without loss of information at 400% zoom without horizontal scrolling.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1. Requires manual testing at 400% zoom.

criteria.name: Non-text Contrast
criteria.description: UI components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1.

criteria.name: Text Spacing
criteria.description: No loss of content when text spacing properties are overridden.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1.

criteria.name: Content on Hover or Focus
criteria.description: Additional content triggered by hover or focus can be dismissed, hovered, and is persistent.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1. Requires interactive testing.

principles.name: Operable
principles.description: User interface components and navigation must be operable.

guidelines.name: Keyboard Accessible
guidelines.description: Make all functionality available from a keyboard.

criteria.name: Keyboard
criteria.description: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface.
criteria.notes: Automated tools can flag non-interactive elements; full keyboard testing requires manual review.

criteria.name: No Keyboard Trap
criteria.description: Keyboard focus can always be moved away from any component.
criteria.notes: Requires manual keyboard navigation to identify traps.

criteria.name: Character Key Shortcuts
criteria.description: Single-character key shortcuts can be turned off, remapped, or activated only on focus.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1.

guidelines.name: Navigable
guidelines.description: Provide ways to help users navigate, find content, and determine where they are.

criteria.name: Bypass Blocks
criteria.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple pages.
criteria.notes: Skip links can be detected; effectiveness requires manual testing.

criteria.name: Focus Order
criteria.description: Focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.
criteria.notes: Tab order can be extracted but logical sequence requires human judgment.

criteria.name: Focus Visible
criteria.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

criteria.name: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
criteria.description: When a component receives keyboard focus, it is not entirely hidden by author-created content.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2.

guidelines.name: Input Modalities
guidelines.description: Make it easier for users to operate functionality through various inputs beyond keyboard.

criteria.name: Pointer Gestures
criteria.description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures has a single-pointer alternative.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1.

criteria.name: Label in Name
criteria.description: For UI components with text labels, the accessible name contains the visible text.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1. Well-supported by axe-core.

criteria.name: Dragging Movements
criteria.description: All functionality that uses dragging movements has a single-pointer alternative.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2.

criteria.name: Target Size (Minimum)
criteria.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24x24 CSS pixels.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2.

principles.name: Understandable
principles.description: Information and the operation of user interface must be understandable.

guidelines.name: Readable
guidelines.description: Make text content readable and understandable.

criteria.name: Language of Page
criteria.description: The default human language of each page can be programmatically determined.
criteria.notes: Easily checked via lang attribute on html element.

criteria.name: Language of Parts
criteria.description: The human language of each passage or phrase can be programmatically determined.

guidelines.name: Predictable
guidelines.description: Make pages appear and operate in predictable ways.

criteria.name: On Focus
criteria.description: Receiving focus does not initiate a change of context.
criteria.notes: Requires interactive keyboard testing.

criteria.name: On Input
criteria.description: Changing a UI component does not automatically cause a change of context.
criteria.notes: Requires interactive testing.

criteria.name: Consistent Help
criteria.description: Help mechanisms appear in the same location across pages.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2.

guidelines.name: Input Assistance
guidelines.description: Help users avoid and correct mistakes.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Input errors are identified and described to the user in text.
criteria.notes: Presence of error messages can be detected; quality requires manual review.

criteria.name: Labels or Instructions
criteria.description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

criteria.name: Redundant Entry
criteria.description: Information previously entered is either auto-populated or available for selection.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2.

criteria.name: Accessible Authentication (Minimum)
criteria.description: Authentication processes do not rely on cognitive function tests unless an alternative is provided.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.2. CAPTCHAs are a common failure.

principles.name: Robust
principles.description: Content must be robust enough to be interpreted by a wide variety of user agents.

guidelines.name: Compatible
guidelines.description: Maximise compatibility with current and future user agents and assistive technologies.

criteria.name: Parsing
criteria.description: In content implemented in markup languages, elements have complete start and end tags and are nested according to their specifications.
criteria.notes: Obsolete in WCAG 2.2 — always passes. Kept for completeness.

criteria.name: Name, Role, Value
criteria.description: For all UI components, the name, role, state, and value can be programmatically determined.
criteria.notes: Core criterion for screen reader compatibility. Partially automatable via axe-core.

criteria.name: Status Messages
criteria.description: Status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties without receiving focus.
criteria.notes: New in WCAG 2.1. ARIA live regions.
